title: Behavior parameterization and lambdas
summary: Modern Java starts by passing behavior through lambdas and functional interfaces so repeated traversal or decision code can be reduced without hiding intent.

title: Stream API pipeline discipline
summary: Stream pipelines support declarative collection processing, but backend use should keep transformations readable and business side effects explicit.

title: Collectors and grouping
summary: Collectors turn stream elements into lists, maps, groups, and custom accumulated results, which is useful for read-model and reporting transformations.

title: Parallel stream risk review
summary: Parallel streams can use multiple cores, but backend services must review shared mutable state, ordering, blocking calls, and measurement before adopting them.

title: Optional as absence boundary
summary: Optional helps model absent lookup results or optional relationships when used consistently at boundaries rather than as a universal replacement for all nulls.

title: Modern date and time modeling
summary: LocalDate, LocalTime, Instant, Duration, and Period support clearer temporal modeling for domain rules, API contracts, and persistence mappings.

title: Default methods and interface evolution
summary: Default methods let interfaces evolve with behavior while preserving compatibility, but backend ports should avoid adding policy by accident.

title: CompletableFuture async composition
summary: CompletableFuture supports asynchronous composition and concurrent calls, but backend use must make executor, timeout, error, and cancellation choices explicit.
